note_data.title: Congruent Triangles
keyPoints: Two triangles are congruent if they have the same size and shape — all corresponding sides and angles are equal
Four conditions for congruence: SSS, SAS, ASA, and RHS (for right triangles only)
SSS: If three sides of one triangle are equal to three sides of another triangle, then triangles are congruent
SAS: If two sides and included angle of one triangle are equal to corresponding parts of another triangle
ASA: If two angles and included side of one triangle are equal to corresponding parts of another triangle
RHS: If hypotenuse and one side of right triangle are equal to hypotenuse and corresponding side of another right triangle
Corresponding parts of congruent triangles are equal (CPCTC)
Symbol for congruence is ≅, read as 'is congruent to'

definitions.definition: Two triangles are said to be congruent if they have the same size and shape, i.e., all corresponding sides and angles are equal.
The parts of two congruent triangles which are equal to each other are called corresponding parts.
If the three sides of one triangle are respectively equal to the three sides of another triangle, then the two triangles are congruent.
If two sides and the included angle of one triangle are respectively equal to two sides and the included angle of another triangle, then the two triangles are congruent.
If two angles and the included side of one triangle are respectively equal to two angles and the included side of another triangle, then the two triangles are congruent.

importantQuestions.question: State the four conditions of congruency of triangles.
importantQuestions.answer: The four conditions are: (1) SSS - Side, Side, Side (2) SAS - Side, Angle, Side (3) ASA - Angle, Side, Angle (4) RHS - Right angle, Hypotenuse, Side

importantQuestions.question: In triangle ABC and triangle PQR, AB = PQ, BC = QR and AC = PR. Prove that triangle ABC ≅ triangle PQR and find the corresponding angles.
importantQuestions.answer: Given: AB = PQ, BC = QR, AC = PR. To Prove: △ABC ≅ △PQR. Proof: In △ABC and △PQR, AB = PQ (given), BC = QR (given), AC = PR (given). Therefore by SSS condition, △ABC ≅ △PQR. Corresponding angles: ∠A = ∠P, ∠B = ∠Q, ∠C = ∠R (CPCTC)

mcqs.question: In which condition of congruency, two sides and included angle are equal?
RHS condition can only be applied to:

commonMistakes: Students confuse similarity with congruency - congruent triangles are equal in size and shape
Writing wrong corresponding vertices when stating congruency - order matters in △ABC ≅ △PQR
Using RHS condition for non-right triangles - it only applies to right triangles
Forgetting to state 'by CPCTC' when finding corresponding parts after proving congruency
Not clearly marking given information and what to prove in geometry proofs
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 long proof question in BSEK papers
Always draw and label diagrams clearly - marks are given for correct diagrams
Learn the exact statements of all four conditions - they are asked word-for-word
Practice writing 'Given, To Prove, Proof' format for all geometry proofs
Remember to write conclusion statement after completing any congruency proof
formulas: If △ABC ≅ △PQR, then AB = PQ, BC = QR, AC = PR and ∠A = ∠P, ∠B = ∠Q, ∠C = ∠R
CPCTC: Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are Equal
note_data.keyDiagram: Draw two triangles ABC and PQR side by side with corresponding sides and angles marked with equal signs. Label all vertices clearly and show which sides/angles are equal using small marks or arrows. This standard diagram appears in most congruency questions.
